description: Verifier confidently CORRECTED a sprinkler spacing value from 12 ft to 15 ft, citing a grounded source. The ground truth is that the original 12 ft was correct for the spec's hazard classification.

finding.issue: Spec specifies 12 ft maximum spacing between sprinkler heads for the office area.

paragraph_map_slice.text: A. Hazard classification: Light Hazard (office occupancy).
B. Maximum sprinkler spacing: 12 ft on center, both directions.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: NFPA 13 allows up to 15 ft maximum spacing for light hazard occupancy in standard-spray sprinklers (NFPA 13 8.6.2.2.1, 2022 edition).

ground_truth.notes: Although NFPA 13 allows up to 15 ft maximum for light hazard, the 12 ft spec value is the project-specified design value (often constrained by sprinkler coverage area, listing, or local AHJ amendment). The model overgeneralized the code allowance into a 'must replace' edit. This is the canonical 'overconfident numeric swap' false positive Chunk 1 is meant to surface.
